files.content:
# Deterministic Retry-Loop Test Plan

This document defines a precise, deterministic test plan for a generic retry loop using a fake clock, a fully specified backoff policy, and an explicit observability contract.

## Policy under test (defaults)
- Operation name: op = "fetchBlob"
- Max attempts (N): 5
- Attempt numbering: 1-based; attempt 1 runs immediately. A successful attempt counts toward N.
- Error classification:
  - retryable: transient error that may succeed on a subsequent attempt
  - non_retryable: permanent error; stop immediately
- Backoff strategy: exponential
  - base_delay_ms = 100
  - factor = 2.0
  - max_delay_ms = 1000 (cap applied per-sleep)
  - jitter = none (deterministic; see Determinism)
- Terminal conditions and timing:
  - success: attempt returns success
  - non_retryable: attempt returns non-retryable error (stop immediately)
  - exhausted: N attempts executed, all retryable failures
  - canceled: cancellation observed at any pre-check or interrupts a sleep
  - deadline: enforced as follows
    - Before initiating any sleep or attempt, compute remaining time budget.
    - If the deadline has elapsed, or remaining_budget < next_delay_ms (for the next sleep), terminate immediately with reason=deadline without starting that sleep/attempt.
    - Sleeps are NOT interrupted by deadline; only cancellation interrupts sleeps. If the deadline elapses during a sleep, the sleep completes normally, and the loop terminates with reason=deadline immediately after waking (before scheduling the next attempt).

Expected backoff schedule (sleep before attempt k>1): [100, 200, 400, 800, 1000, 1000, ...] ms (capped at 1000ms). Number of sleeps equals the number of preceding retryable failures.

## Determinism and timing
- All time is controlled by a fake clock; no wall-clock usage in tests.
- Sleeps advance the fake clock exactly by the scheduled delay (no jitter).
- Attempt execution time may be modeled as instantaneous (0ms) unless the test explicitly stubs a duration.

## Observability contract
Metrics (Prometheus-style):
- rl_attempt_total (counter)
  - Labels: op (string), outcome ("success"|"error"), error_kind ("retryable"|"non_retryable"|"none")
  - Increments once per attempt.
- rl_termination_total (counter)
  - Labels: op (string), reason ("success"|"exhausted"|"non_retryable"|"canceled"|"deadline")
  - Increments once per retry loop, exactly at termination.

Structured logs (logger: retry.loop). Each entry is a JSON object with fields:
- ts_ms (int): fake clock timestamp in milliseconds at emission
- op (string)
- attempt (int):
  - For attempt_start/attempt_result: 1-based index of the attempt being run.
  - For before_sleep: index of the attempt that just completed (i.e., the one that produced the retryable error).
  - For loop_terminated: last executed attempt index; if no attempts executed, attempt = 0.
- event ("attempt_start"|"attempt_result"|"before_sleep"|"loop_terminated")
- delay_ms (int|null): present for before_sleep; otherwise null
- interrupted (bool|null): present for before_sleep; true if the sleep ended due to cancellation; false if the full delay elapsed
- outcome ("success"|"error"|null): set for attempt_result; otherwise null
- error_kind ("retryable"|"non_retryable"|"none"|null): set for attempt_result; "none" on success
- reason ("success"|"exhausted"|"non_retryable"|"canceled"|"deadline"|null): set for loop_terminated; otherwise null

## Test matrix and acceptance criteria
Unless noted, tests use the default policy above and a zero-duration attempt execution.

1) Immediate success on first attempt
- Setup: Attempt 1 returns success.
- Expected:
  - Attempts: 1; Sleeps: 0
  - Metrics:
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="success",error_kind="none"} += 1
    - rl_termination_total{op="fetchBlob",reason="success"} += 1
  - Logs (sequence):
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:1}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:1, outcome:"success", error_kind:"none"}
    - {event:"loop_terminated", attempt:1, reason:"success"}

2) Success on 3rd attempt after retryable failures
- Setup: Attempt 1 -> retryable error; Attempt 2 -> retryable error; Attempt 3 -> success.
- Expected delays: [100, 200] ms between attempts (fake clock advances 100 then 200)
- Expected:
  - Attempts: 3; Sleeps: 2
  - Metrics:
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="error",error_kind="retryable"} += 2
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="success",error_kind="none"} += 1
    - rl_termination_total{op="fetchBlob",reason="success"} += 1
  - Logs:
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:1}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:1, outcome:"error", error_kind:"retryable"}
    - {event:"before_sleep", attempt:1, delay_ms:100, interrupted:false}
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:2}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:2, outcome:"error", error_kind:"retryable"}
    - {event:"before_sleep", attempt:2, delay_ms:200, interrupted:false}
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:3}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:3, outcome:"success", error_kind:"none"}
    - {event:"loop_terminated", attempt:3, reason:"success"}

3) Non-retryable error on first attempt
- Setup: Attempt 1 -> non_retryable error.
- Expected:
  - Attempts: 1; Sleeps: 0; Termination: non_retryable
  - Metrics:
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="error",error_kind="non_retryable"} += 1
    - rl_termination_total{op="fetchBlob",reason="non_retryable"} += 1
  - Logs:
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:1}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:1, outcome:"error", error_kind:"non_retryable"}
    - {event:"loop_terminated", attempt:1, reason:"non_retryable"}

4) Exhausted after N retryable failures
- Setup: All N attempts -> retryable error (N=5).
- Expected delays before attempts 2..N: [100, 200, 400, 800]
- Expected:
  - Attempts: 5; Sleeps: 4; Termination: exhausted
  - Metrics:
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="error",error_kind="retryable"} += 5
    - rl_termination_total{op="fetchBlob",reason="exhausted"} += 1
  - Logs (pattern):
    - attempt_start/result(1,error,retryable)
    - before_sleep(100,interrupted:false)
    - attempt_start/result(2,error,retryable)
    - before_sleep(200,interrupted:false)
    - attempt_start/result(3,error,retryable)
    - before_sleep(400,interrupted:false)
    - attempt_start/result(4,error,retryable)
    - before_sleep(800,interrupted:false)
    - attempt_start/result(5,error,retryable)
    - loop_terminated(attempt:5, reason:"exhausted")

5) Cancellation during sleep after attempt 2
- Setup: Attempts 1 and 2 -> retryable errors; cancellation is triggered during the sleep that follows attempt 2.
- Expected:
  - Sleeps executed: first sleep 100ms fully completes; second sleep is interrupted by cancellation and does not complete to 200ms
  - Attempts: 2; Termination: canceled (during sleep after attempt 2)
  - Metrics:
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="error",error_kind="retryable"} += 2
    - rl_termination_total{op="fetchBlob",reason="canceled"} += 1
  - Logs:
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:1}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:1, outcome:"error", error_kind:"retryable"}
    - {event:"before_sleep", attempt:1, delay_ms:100, interrupted:false}
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:2}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:2, outcome:"error", error_kind:"retryable"}
    - {event:"before_sleep", attempt:2, delay_ms:200, interrupted:true}
    - {event:"loop_terminated", attempt:2, reason:"canceled"}

6) Deadline reached before scheduling attempt 3
- Setup: Deadline = 250ms after start. Attempt 1 -> retryable error; first sleep 100ms completes (t=100); Attempt 2 -> retryable error; next scheduled sleep would be 200ms, which would exceed remaining budget (150ms < 200ms).
- Expected:
  - Attempts: 2; No second sleep begins; Termination: deadline
  - Fake clock timeline: t=0 start; t=100 after first sleep; termination at t=100 with reason=deadline (before starting the second sleep)
  - Metrics:
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="error",error_kind="retryable"} += 2
    - rl_termination_total{op="fetchBlob",reason="deadline"} += 1
  - Logs:
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:1}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:1, outcome:"error", error_kind:"retryable"}
    - {event:"before_sleep", attempt:1, delay_ms:100, interrupted:false}
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:2}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:2, outcome:"error", error_kind:"retryable"}
    - {event:"loop_terminated", attempt:2, reason:"deadline"}

7) Backoff cap applied (policy override for this test only)
- Policy override: N=6 (other values unchanged)
- Setup: Attempts 1..5 -> retryable error; Attempt 6 -> success.
- Expected delays before attempts 2..6: [100, 200, 400, 800, 1000] (5th delay capped at 1000ms, not 1600ms)
- Expected:
  - Attempts: 6; Sleeps: 5
  - Metrics:
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="error",error_kind="retryable"} += 5
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="success",error_kind="none"} += 1
    - rl_termination_total{op="fetchBlob",reason="success"} += 1
  - Logs include before_sleep entries with delay_ms exactly [100,200,400,800,1000] (all interrupted:false), and final loop_terminated(attempt:6, reason:"success").

8) Immediate cancellation before the first attempt begins
- Setup: Cancellation is already signaled at t=0 before attempt 1 is started.
- Expected:
  - Attempts: 0; Sleeps: 0; Termination: canceled
  - Metrics:
    - rl_termination_total{op="fetchBlob",reason="canceled"} += 1
  - Logs:
    - {event:"loop_terminated", attempt:0, reason:"canceled"}

9) Deadline elapses during sleep (non-interruptible)
- Setup: Deadline = 150ms after start. Attempt 1 -> retryable error; first sleep 100ms completes at t=100. Attempt 2 -> retryable error; second sleep is 200ms starting at t=100. Deadline elapses at t=150 during this sleep.
- Expected semantics: Sleeps are not interrupted by deadline, so the second sleep completes at t=300; upon waking, the loop terminates with reason=deadline before scheduling attempt 3.
- Expected:
  - Attempts: 2; Sleeps: 2; Termination: deadline (post-sleep)
  - Metrics:
    - rl_attempt_total{op="fetchBlob",outcome="error",error_kind="retryable"} += 2
    - rl_termination_total{op="fetchBlob",reason="deadline"} += 1
  - Logs:
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:1}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:1, outcome:"error", error_kind:"retryable"}
    - {event:"before_sleep", attempt:1, delay_ms:100, interrupted:false}
    - {event:"attempt_start", attempt:2}
    - {event:"attempt_result", attempt:2, outcome:"error", error_kind:"retryable"}
    - {event:"before_sleep", attempt:2, delay_ms:200, interrupted:false}
    - {event:"loop_terminated", attempt:2, reason:"deadline"}

## Notes
- The fake clock must be the single source of time for the retry loop and the test assertions.
- No randomness or jitter is permitted in these tests; all delays are deterministic.
- Error helpers used by the harness should expose error_kind in {retryable, non_retryable} explicitly.
- Verification must assert both behavior (attempt counts, delays, termination) and observability (exact metrics increments and log sequences).
- loop_terminated.attempt is always the last executed attempt index; when no attempts executed, it is 0 as an explicit sentinel.
- File ends cleanly.
'}],
